children.text: $219,800,000,000, to remain available through (except as provided in subsection (c)(5)), for making payments under this section to States, territories, and Tribal governments to mitigate the fiscal effects stemming from the public health emergency with respect to the Coronavirus Disease (COVID–19); andDecember 31, 2024
$50,000,000, to remain available until expended, for the costs of the Secretary for administration of the funds established under this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $4,500,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (a)(1) to make payments to the territories.

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of such amount shall be allocated by the Secretary equally to each territory; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent of such amount shall be allocated by the Secretary as an additional amount to each territory in an amount which bears the same proportion to ½ of the total amount reserved under subparagraph (A) as the population of the territory bears to the total population of all such territories.

children.heading: Payment
children.label: C Payment
children.text: The Secretary shall pay each territory the total of the amounts allocated for the territory under subparagraph (B) in accordance with paragraph (6).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $20,000,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (a)(1) to make payments to Tribal governments.

children.label: i
children.text: $1,000,000,000 shall be allocated by the Secretary equally among each of the Tribal governments; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $19,000,000,000 shall be allocated by the Secretary to the Tribal governments in a manner determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment
children.label: C Payment
children.text: The Secretary shall pay each Tribal government the total of the amounts allocated for the Tribal government under subparagraph (B) in accordance with paragraph (6).

children.heading: Payments to each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia
children.label: 3 Payments to each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $195,300,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (a)(1) to make payments to each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia.

children.label: i
children.text: $25,500,000,000 of such amount shall be allocated by the Secretary equally among each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia;

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount equal to $1,250,000,000 less the amount allocated for the District of Columbia pursuant to shall be allocated by the Secretary as an additional amount to the District of Columbia; andsection 801(c)(6) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: an amount equal to the remainder of the amount reserved under subparagraph (A) after the application of clauses (i) and (ii) of this subparagraph shall be allocated by the Secretary as an additional amount to each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia in an amount which bears the same proportion to such remainder as the average estimated number of seasonally-adjusted unemployed individuals (as measured by the Bureau of Labor Statistics Local Area Unemployment Statistics program) in the State or District of Columbia over the 3-month period ending with December 2020 bears to the average estimated number of seasonally-adjusted unemployed individuals in all of the 50 States and the District of Columbia over the same period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary shall pay each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia, from the amount reserved under subparagraph (A), the total of the amounts allocated for the State and District of Columbia under subparagraph (B) in accordance with paragraph (6).

children.label: aa
children.text: the total amounts allocated for 1 of the 50 States or the District of Columbia under subparagraph (B) (as determined without regard to this clause); and

children.label: bb
children.text: the amounts allocated under to the State (for distribution by the State to nonentitlement units of local government in the State) and to metropolitan cities and counties in the State;section 803 of this title

children.heading: Pro rata adjustment
children.label: II Pro rata adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust on a pro rata basis the amount of the allocations for each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia determined under subparagraph (B)(iii) (without regard to this clause) to the extent necessary to comply with the requirement of subclause (I).

children.heading: Pro rata adjustment authority
children.label: 4 Pro rata adjustment authority
children.text: The amounts otherwise determined for allocation and payment under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) may be adjusted by the Secretary on a pro rata basis to the extent necessary to ensure that all available funds are allocated to States, territories, and Tribal governments in accordance with the requirements specified in each such paragraph (as applicable).

children.heading: Population data
children.label: 5 Population data
children.text: For purposes of determining allocations for a territory under this section, the population of the territory shall be determined based on the most recent data available from the Bureau of the Census.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To the extent practicable, subject to clause (ii), with respect to each State and territory allocated a payment under this subsection, the Secretary shall make the payment required for the State or territory not later than 60 days after the date on which the certification required under subsection (d)(1) is provided to the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall have the authority to withhold payment of up to 50 percent of the amount allocated to each State and territory (other than payment of the amount allocated under paragraph (3)(B)(ii) to the District of Columbia) for a period of up to 12 months from the date on which the State or territory provides the certification required under subsection (d)(1). The Secretary shall exercise such authority with respect to a State or territory based on the unemployment rate in the State or territory as of such date.

children.heading: Payment of withheld amount
children.label: II Payment of withheld amount
children.text: Before paying to a State or territory the remainder of an amount allocated to the State or territory (subject to subclause (III)) that has been withheld by the Secretary under subclause (I), the Secretary shall require the State or territory to submit a second certification under subsection (d)(1), in addition to such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Recovery of amounts subject to recoupment
children.label: III Recovery of amounts subject to recoupment
children.text: If a State or territory is required under subsection (e) to repay funds for failing to comply with subsection (c), the Secretary may reduce the amount otherwise payable to the State or territory under subclause (II) by the amount that the State or territory would otherwise be required to repay under such subsection (e).

children.heading: Tribal governments
children.label: B Tribal governments
children.text: To the extent practicable, with respect to each Tribal government for which an amount is allocated under this subsection, the Secretary shall make the payment required for the Tribal government not later than 60 days after .March 11, 2021

children.heading: Initial payment to District of Columbia
children.label: C Initial payment to District of Columbia
children.text: The Secretary shall pay the amount allocated under paragraph (3)(B)(ii) to the District of Columbia not later than 15 days after .March 11, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: to respond to the public health emergency with respect to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID–19) or its negative economic impacts, including assistance to households, small businesses, and nonprofits, or aid to impacted industries such as tourism, travel, and hospitality;

children.label: B
children.text: to respond to workers performing essential work during the COVID–19 public health emergency by providing premium pay to eligible workers of the State, territory, or Tribal government that are performing such essential work, or by providing grants to eligible employers that have eligible workers who perform essential work;

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the reduction in revenue of such State, territory, or Tribal government due to the COVID–19 public health emergency relative to revenues collected in the most recent full fiscal year of the State, territory, or Tribal government prior to the emergency; or

children.label: D
children.text: to make necessary investments in water, sewer, or broadband infrastructure; or

children.label: E
children.text: to provide emergency relief from natural disasters or the negative economic impacts of natural disasters, including temporary emergency housing, food assistance, financial assistance for lost wages, or other immediate needs.

children.heading: Further restriction on use of funds
children.label: 2 Further restriction on use of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State or territory shall not use the funds provided under this section or transferred pursuant to to either directly or indirectly offset a reduction in the net tax revenue of such State or territory resulting from a change in law, regulation, or administrative interpretation during the covered period that reduces any tax (by providing for a reduction in a rate, a rebate, a deduction, a credit, or otherwise) or delays the imposition of any tax or tax increase.section 803(c)(4) of this title

children.heading: Pension funds
children.label: B Pension funds
children.text: No State or territory may use funds made available under this section for deposit into any pension fund.

children.heading: Transfer authority
children.label: 3 Transfer authority
children.text: A State, territory, or Tribal government receiving a payment from funds made available under this section may transfer funds to a private nonprofit organization (as that term is defined in ), a Tribal organization (as that term is defined in ), a public benefit corporation involved in the transportation of passengers or cargo, or a special-purpose unit of State or local government.section 11360 of this titlesection 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Use of funds to satisfy non-federal matching requirements for authorized Bureau of Reclamation water projects
children.label: 4 Use of funds to satisfy non-federal matching requirements for authorized Bureau of Reclamation water projects
children.text: Funds provided under this section for an authorized Bureau of Reclamation project may be used for purposes of satisfying any non-Federal matching requirement required for the project.

children.heading: Authority to use funds for certain infrastructure projects
children.label: 5 Authority to use funds for certain infrastructure projects
i
children.text: in the case of a project eligible under or section 5309 or 6701 of title 49 to satisfy a non-Federal share requirement applicable to such a project; andsection 117 of title 23

children.label: I
children.text: to satisfy a non-Federal share requirement applicable to such a project; and

children.label: II
children.text: to repay a loan provided under such program.

children.label: i
children.text: A project eligible under .section 117 of title 23

children.label: ii
children.text: A project eligible under .section 119 of title 23

children.label: iii
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 124 of title 23

children.label: iv
children.text: A project eligible under .section 133 of title 23

children.label: v
children.text: An activity to carry out .section 134 of title 23

children.label: vi
children.text: A project eligible under .section 148 of title 23

children.label: vii
children.text: A project eligible under .section 149 of title 23

children.label: viii
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 151(f) of title 23

children.label: ix
children.text: A project eligible under .section 165 of title 23

children.label: x
children.text: A project eligible under .section 167 of title 23

children.label: xi
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 173 of title 23

children.label: xii
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 175 of title 23

children.label: xiii
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 176 of title 23

children.label: xiv
children.text: A project eligible under .section 202 of title 23

children.label: xv
children.text: A project eligible under .section 203 of title 23

children.label: xvi
children.text: A project eligible under .section 204 of title 23

children.label: xvii
children.text: A project eligible under the program for national infrastructure investments (commonly known as the “Rebuilding American Infrastructure with Sustainability and Equity (RAISE) grant program”).

children.label: xviii
children.text: A project eligible for credit assistance under the TIFIA program under chapter 6 of title 23.

children.label: xix
children.text: A project that furthers the completion of a designated route of the Appalachian Development Highway System under .section 14501 of title 40

children.label: xx
children.text: A project eligible under .section 5307 of title 49

children.label: xxi
children.text: A project eligible under .section 5309 of title 49

children.label: xxii
children.text: A project eligible under .section 5311 of title 49

children.label: xxiii
children.text: A project eligible under .section 5337 of title 49

children.label: xxiv
children.text: A project eligible under .section 5339 of title 49

children.label: xxv
children.text: A project eligible under , as added by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.section 6703 of title 49

children.label: xxvi
children.text: A project eligible under title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: xxvii
children.text: A project eligible under the bridge replacement, rehabilitation, preservation, protection, and construction program under paragraph (1) under the heading “HIGHWAY INFRASTRUCTURE PROGRAM” under the heading “FEDERAL HIGHWAY ADMINISTRATION” under the heading “DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION” under title VIII of division J of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.

children.heading: Limitation on amounts to be used for infrastructure projects
children.label: i Limitation on amounts to be used for infrastructure projects
bb
children.text: 30 percent of such payment.

children.heading: Rule of application
children.label: II Rule of application
children.text: The spending limitation under subclause (I) shall not apply to any use of funds permitted under paragraph (1), and any such use of funds shall be disregarded for purposes of applying such spending limitation.

children.heading: Limitation on operating expenses
children.label: ii Limitation on operating expenses
children.text: Funds provided under a payment made under this section shall not be used for operating expenses of a project described in clauses (xx) through (xxiv) of subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: the requirements of section 60102 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act [] shall apply to funds provided under a payment made under this section that are used pursuant to subparagraph (A) for a project described in clause (xxvi) of subparagraph (B) that relates to broadband infrastructure;47 U.S.C. 1702

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of titles 23, 40, and 49, title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 ( et seq.), and the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et. seq) shall apply to funds provided under a payment made under this section that are used for projects described in subparagraph (B); and42 U.S.C. 530142 U.S.C. 43211So in original.1

children.label: aa
children.text: demonstrate progress in achieving a state of good repair as required by the State’s asset management plan under ; andsection 119(e) of title 23

children.label: bb
children.text: support the achievement of 1 or more performance targets of the State established under .section 150 of title 23

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: iv Oversight
children.text: The Secretary may delegate oversight and administration of the requirements described in clause (iii) to the appropriate Federal agency.

children.heading: Supplement, not supplant
children.label: v Supplement, not supplant
children.text: Amounts from a payment made under this section that are used by a State, territory, or Tribal government for uses described in subparagraph (A) shall supplement, and not supplant, other Federal, State, territorial, Tribal, and local government funds (as applicable) otherwise available for such uses.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: D Reports
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, shall provide periodic reports on the use of funds by States, territories, and Tribal governments under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Availability
children.label: E Availability
children.text: Funds provided under a payment made under this section to a State, territory, or Tribal government shall remain available for obligation for a use described in subparagraph (A) through , except that no amount of such funds may be expended after .December 31, 2024September 30, 2026

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In order for a State or territory to receive a payment under this section, or a transfer of funds under , the State or territory shall provide the Secretary with a certification, signed by an authorized officer of such State or territory, that such State or territory requires the payment or transfer to carry out the activities specified in subsection (c) of this section and will use any payment under this section, or transfer of funds under , in compliance with subsection (c) of this section.section 803(c)(4) of this titlesection 803(c)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the uses of funds by such State, territory, or Tribal government, including, in the case of a State or a territory, all modifications to the State’s or territory’s tax revenue sources during the covered period; and

children.label: B
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require for the administration of this section.
the amount of the applicable reduction to net tax revenue attributable to such violation; and
the amount of funds received by such State or territory pursuant to a payment made under this section or a transfer made under .section 803(c)(4) of this title

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall have the authority to issue such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: begins on ; andMarch 3, 2021

children.label: B
children.text: ends on the last day of the fiscal year of such State, territory, or Tribal government in which all funds received by the State, territory, or Tribal government from a payment made under this section or a transfer made under have been expended or returned to, or recovered by, the Secretary.section 803(c)(4) of this title

children.heading: Eligible workers
children.label: 2 Eligible workers
children.text: The term “eligible workers” means those workers needed to maintain continuity of operations of essential critical infrastructure sectors and additional sectors as each Governor of a State or territory, or each Tribal government, may designate as critical to protect the health and well-being of the residents of their State, territory, or Tribal government.

children.heading: Premium pay
children.label: 3 Premium pay
children.text: The term “premium pay” means an amount of up to $13 per hour that is paid to an eligible worker, in addition to wages or remuneration the eligible worker otherwise receives, for all work performed by the eligible worker during the COVID–19 public health emergency. Such amount may not exceed $25,000 with respect to any single eligible worker.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Territory
children.label: 6 Territory
children.text: The term “territory” means the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.

children.heading: Tribal Government
children.label: 7 Tribal Government
children.text: The term “Tribal Government” means the recognized governing body of any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, individually identified (including parenthetically) in the list published most recently as of , pursuant to .March 11, 2021section 5131 of title 25
